author: lodekeeper
content:
BACKLOG, memory, and index all updated. The fix is pushed and verified; CI is queued pending maintainer approval. Reporting in-thread.

Yeah, that one's on me. The `macos-arm64` fail wasn't the `beacon_engine.nim` change — that compiled clean, the `V5/V6` raiseAssert arm satisfies the exhaustiveness check. It was the regression test I added:

`test_engine_api.nim(321) Error: undeclared field: 'txPool' for type TestEnv`

`TestEnv` only holds com/server/client/chain — `setupEnv` builds the txpool locally and never hangs it off the env, so `env.txPool` never existed. Fixed by building one the way setupEnv does: `BeaconEngineRef.new(env.txPool)` → `BeaconEngineRef.new(TxPoolRef.new(env.chain))`. Pushed as `8190bc8`.

This time I traced it through the real web3 defs (`targetGasLimit`→V4 in execution_types, `WithdrawalV1`/`ExecutionPayload.withdrawals` fields, `w3Qty` overloads) and audited every symbol in the test proc — not just the one line CI flagged — so there shouldn't be a second error hiding behind the first. Fair warning though: I still can't run a Nim compiler on this box, so CI's the real judge.

@nflaig the new run is sitting on `action_required` (fork PR needs a maintainer to approve workflows) — mind hitting "Approve and run" when you get a sec?

author: tersec
content: jangko merged lodekeeper's PR, should take effect within a couple of hours
